install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  ADMIN_TOKEN: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
    - name: websocket
      port: 3012
      targetPort: 3012
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 65534
        runAsGroup: 65534
        fsGroup: 65534
      containers:
        - name: vaultwarden
          image: vaultwarden/server:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80
            - name: websocket
              containerPort: 3012
          env:
            - name: DOMAIN
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: SIGNUPS_ALLOWED
              value: "true"
            - name: WEBSOCKET_ENABLED
              value: "true"
            - name: ADMIN_TOKEN
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: ADMIN_TOKEN
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /alive
              port: 80
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /alive
              port: 80
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 80

secrets.label: Admin panel token
secrets.description: Token that protects the /admin panel (the password to log in at https://<hostname>/admin). Vaultwarden also accepts an Argon2 PHC hash here. Disable SIGNUPS_ALLOWED after creating your first user.

name: Vaultwarden
tagline: Lightweight Bitwarden-compatible password manager.
description: Unofficial Bitwarden server written in Rust. Single binary, SQLite by default, drop-in compatible with the official Bitwarden clients. Ideal first install — small footprint, no external database required.
notes: Set an ADMIN_TOKEN env var to enable the /admin panel. Disable SIGNUPS_ALLOWED after creating your first user.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Vaultwarden ({{instance}}) — Bitwarden-compatible password manager.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Persistent storage: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Single Deployment + Service + Ingress + PersistentVolumeClaim, one YAML document each separated by `---`.
- Image: `vaultwarden/server:latest`, `imagePullPolicy: Always`.
- 1 replica (SQLite — no HA).
- Strategy: `Recreate` (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 65534`, `runAsGroup: 65534`, `fsGroup: 65534`. Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: false` (Vaultwarden writes to /data). Drop all caps.
- Env: `DOMAIN=https://{{hostname}}`, `SIGNUPS_ALLOWED=true` (user will lock down later), `WEBSOCKET_ENABLED=true`.
- PVC: `{{storage}}Gi`, default storageClass (leave unset).
- Container port 80 (HTTP) and 3012 (WebSocket).
- Service: ClusterIP, expose 80 and 3012.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests cpu 100m / mem 128Mi, limits cpu 500m / mem 512Mi.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: vaultwarden` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet path=/alive port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 10.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention even though Docker Hub is public).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest. No prose outside the fence. No comments inside the YAML unless absolutely necessary. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

name: Supabase
tagline: Open-source Firebase — Postgres, Auth, Storage, Realtime.
description: Bundled backend: Postgres + GoTrue auth + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage + Kong API gateway + Studio admin UI. Large surface area — multiple Deployments and Services. Expect ~2–4 GiB memory usage at idle.
notes: Multi-service install. Studio admin UI is exposed via the ingress; the Postgres + GoTrue + PostgREST services stay ClusterIP only. Generate JWT secret + anon/service keys before applying.
installPromptTemplate:
You are generating a Kubernetes manifest for the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Supabase ({{instance}}) — open-source Firebase alternative. Self-hosted bundle: Postgres + GoTrue auth + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage + Kong API gateway + Studio.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Public ingress hostname: {{hostname}} (Studio admin UI)
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Postgres PVC size: {{storage}}Gi
- Extra notes from user: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions (MUST follow)
- Generate placeholders for the secrets the user MUST fill in before applying: `JWT_SECRET` (32+ char random), `ANON_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=anon), `SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` (JWT signed with JWT_SECRET, role=service_role), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `DASHBOARD_USERNAME`, `DASHBOARD_PASSWORD`. Surface these as `valueFrom: secretKeyRef` against a single Secret named `{{instance}}-secrets` — and include that Secret as the FIRST document with placeholder values clearly labeled `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- All Deployments: 1 replica, `Recreate` strategy (PVC is RWO).
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Postgres uses `runAsUser: 999` (postgres uid in supabase/postgres image).
- Postgres: image `supabase/postgres:15.1.0.147`, PVC `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/var/lib/postgresql/data` (subPath `pgdata`).
- GoTrue: image `supabase/gotrue:v2.151.0`, env wired to Postgres via service DNS.
- PostgREST: image `postgrest/postgrest:v12.0.1`.
- Realtime: image `supabase/realtime:v2.25.66`.
- Storage: image `supabase/storage-api:v0.43.11`, PVC `5Gi` for file storage.
- Kong: image `kong:2.8.1`, declarative config via ConfigMap routing `/auth → gotrue:9999`, `/rest → postgrest:3000`, `/storage → storage:5000`, `/realtime → realtime:4000`.
- Studio: image `supabase/studio:20240729-ce42139`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Only one Ingress, pointing to Studio on port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. DO NOT set `router.tls: "true"`. TLS section: `hosts: [{{hostname}}]`, `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources per service: requests 100–300m / 256–512Mi, limits 1 / 1Gi. Postgres gets more: 500m / 1Gi requests, 2 / 4Gi limits.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: supabase`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <postgres|gotrue|postgrest|realtime|storage|kong|studio>`.
- Image pull secret: `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` on every pod spec.
- Readiness probes on each service appropriate to the image (postgres: `pg_isready`; HTTP services: `httpGet /health` or `/`).

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full manifest as multiple `---`-separated documents. No prose outside the fence. The first document MUST be the placeholder `{{instance}}-secrets` Secret with `<FILL_ME_IN>` markers and a top comment instructing the user to fill before applying. The YAML must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -` once secrets are filled.

install.manifest:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  SECRET_KEY_BASE: <FILL_ME_IN>
  TOTP_VAULT_KEY: <FILL_ME_IN>
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
  CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-clickhouse
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-config
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
data:
  BASE_URL: https://{{hostname}}
  DISABLE_REGISTRATION: invite_only
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: plausible
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: plausible
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "plausible", "-d", "plausible"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: clickhouse
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8123
      targetPort: 8123
    - name: native
      port: 9000
      targetPort: 9000
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-clickhouse-config
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
data:
  low-resources.xml: |
    <clickhouse>
      <logger>
        <level>warning</level>
        <console>true</console>
      </logger>
      <query_thread_log remove="remove"/>
      <query_log remove="remove"/>
      <text_log remove="remove"/>
      <trace_log remove="remove"/>
      <metric_log remove="remove"/>
      <asynchronous_metric_log remove="remove"/>
      <session_log remove="remove"/>
      <part_log remove="remove"/>
    </clickhouse>
  user-config.xml: |
    <clickhouse>
      <profiles>
        <default>
          <log_queries>0</log_queries>
          <log_query_threads>0</log_query_threads>
        </default>
      </profiles>
    </clickhouse>
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: clickhouse
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
spec:
  serviceName: clickhouse
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: clickhouse
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 101
        fsGroup: 101
      containers:
        - name: clickhouse
          image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine
          env:
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_DB
              value: plausible_events_db
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_USER
              value: plausible
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8123
            - name: native
              containerPort: 9000
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /ping
              port: 8123
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/clickhouse
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/clickhouse-server/config.d/low-resources.xml
              subPath: low-resources.xml
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/clickhouse-server/users.d/user-config.xml
              subPath: user-config.xml
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: {{instance}}-clickhouse-config
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-clickhouse
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8000
      targetPort: 8000
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      nodeName: {{nodeName}}
      imagePullSecrets:
        - name: {{imagePullSecret}}
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          command: ["sh", "-c", "until pg_isready -h postgres -U plausible; do echo waiting for postgres; sleep 3; done"]
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
        - name: migrate
          image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.4
          args: ["db", "migrate"]
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: {{instance}}-config
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
            - name: SECRET_KEY_BASE
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY_BASE
            - name: TOTP_VAULT_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: TOTP_VAULT_KEY
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgresql://plausible:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/plausible
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE_URL
              value: http://plausible:$(CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD)@clickhouse:8123/plausible_events_db
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: plausible
          image: ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.4
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: {{instance}}-config
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD
            - name: SECRET_KEY_BASE
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY_BASE
            - name: TOTP_VAULT_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: TOTP_VAULT_KEY
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgresql://plausible:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/plausible
            - name: CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE_URL
              value: http://plausible:$(CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD)@clickhouse:8123/plausible_events_db
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8000
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            limits:
              cpu: 2000m
              memory: 3Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /api/health
              port: 8000
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: plausible
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 8000

secrets.label: Secret key base
secrets.description: Phoenix signing key for sessions and cookies. Must be a 64-character random string (openssl rand -base64 48).

secrets.label: TOTP vault key
secrets.description: Encryption key for two-factor auth secrets, a 32-byte random value (openssl rand -base64 32).

secrets.label: Postgres password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled Postgres database (settings + users).

secrets.label: ClickHouse password
secrets.description: Password for the bundled ClickHouse database (analytics events).

name: Plausible
tagline: Lightweight, cookie-free web analytics.
description: Privacy-focused Google Analytics alternative. Self-hosted Plausible Analytics dashboard backed by Postgres and ClickHouse. Open source, GDPR-friendly, simple stats UI.
notes: Plausible needs both Postgres (settings + users) AND ClickHouse (events). Bundle the dependencies as StatefulSets with their own PVCs. SECRET_KEY_BASE must be a 64-char base64 string.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Plausible Analytics** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: 5Gi; ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Cluster conventions
- Three workloads: `plausible` (the Phoenix app), `postgres` (StatefulSet), `clickhouse` (StatefulSet).
- Plausible image: `ghcr.io/plausible/community-edition:v2.1.4`.
- Postgres image: `postgres:16-alpine`, ClickHouse image: `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`.
- Secret named `{{instance}}-secrets` with `SECRET_KEY_BASE` (64-char base64 placeholder), `TOTP_VAULT_KEY` (32-byte placeholder), Postgres password, ClickHouse password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- ConfigMap for env: `BASE_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://...`, `CLICKHOUSE_DATABASE_URL=http://...`, `DISABLE_REGISTRATION=invite_only`.
- One replica per workload, `Recreate` strategy, PVCs default storageClass.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. ClickHouse needs `runAsUser: 101`; Postgres `runAsUser: 999`.
- Services: all ClusterIP. Plausible service on port 8000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Sentry** ({{instance}}) self-hosted on the user's k3s cluster.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}}
- Postgres PVC: {{storage}}Gi; ClickHouse PVC: 30Gi; Kafka PVC: 10Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads (one Deployment each unless noted): `sentry-web` (image `getsentry/sentry:25.1.0`, runs `sentry run web`), `sentry-worker` (`sentry run worker`), `sentry-cron` (`sentry run cron`), `sentry-ingest` (`sentry run consumer ingest-events`), `snuba-api` (image `getsentry/snuba:25.1.0`), `snuba-consumer` (`snuba consumer --storage errors`), `relay` (image `getsentry/relay:25.1.0`). Plus dependency StatefulSets: `postgres:15-alpine`, `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3-alpine`, `redis:7-alpine`, `apache/kafka:3.9.1` (Kafka in KRaft mode — no ZooKeeper), `memcached:1.6-alpine`.
- Launch the Sentry/Snuba/Relay workloads via `args:` so each image's docker-entrypoint and its `sentry`/`snuba` launcher are preserved — e.g. `args: ["run","web"]`, `args: ["run","worker"]`, `args: ["run","cron"]`, `args: ["run","consumer","ingest-events"]` for getsentry/sentry; `args: ["api"]` and `args: ["consumer","--storage","errors"]` for getsentry/snuba; `args: ["run"]` for getsentry/relay. Do NOT override `command:`.
- Kafka runs as a single-node KRaft broker (image `apache/kafka:3.9.1`, no ZooKeeper). Configure it purely through env (do NOT set command/args): `KAFKA_NODE_ID=1`, `KAFKA_PROCESS_ROLES=broker,controller`, `KAFKA_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://:9092,CONTROLLER://:9093`, `KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS=PLAINTEXT://kafka:9092`, `KAFKA_LISTENER_SECURITY_PROTOCOL_MAP=CONTROLLER:PLAINTEXT,PLAINTEXT:PLAINTEXT`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_LISTENER_NAMES=CONTROLLER`, `KAFKA_CONTROLLER_QUORUM_VOTERS=1@kafka:9093`, `KAFKA_OFFSETS_TOPIC_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_REPLICATION_FACTOR=1`, `KAFKA_TRANSACTION_STATE_LOG_MIN_ISR=1`, `KAFKA_GROUP_INITIAL_REBALANCE_DELAY_MS=0`, `KAFKA_LOG_DIRS=/var/lib/kafka/data`, and a fixed `CLUSTER_ID` (a stable UUID; add a comment that it must not change for the life of the volume). The Kafka StatefulSet's Service MUST be named `kafka` exposing port 9092 (so `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092` resolves) and 9093 for the controller quorum; mount the Kafka PVC at `/var/lib/kafka/data`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets`: `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` (50-char `<FILL_ME_IN>`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD`, `CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD`, initial admin email + password — all `<FILL_ME_IN>`.
- Env on every sentry-* pod: `SENTRY_REDIS_HOST=redis`, `SENTRY_POSTGRES_HOST=postgres`, `SENTRY_KAFKA_HOST=kafka:9092`, `SENTRY_DB_NAME=sentry`, `SENTRY_DB_USER=sentry`, `SENTRY_SECRET_KEY` from secretKeyRef.
- 1 replica per workload (HA is out of scope), `Recreate` strategy.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, drop all caps. Sentry image runs as user 999.
- Services: ClusterIP for everything; only `sentry-web` (port 9000) is ingressed.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: web/worker each 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem. Snuba and Kafka 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/3Gi mem. ClickHouse 1/4 cpu, 2Gi/4Gi mem. Postgres 500m/2 cpu, 1Gi/2Gi mem.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: sentry`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <web|worker|cron|ingest|snuba-api|snuba-consumer|relay|postgres|clickhouse|redis|kafka|memcached>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` on every pod spec.
- A `sentry-upgrade` Job (Helm-chart-style) that runs `sentry upgrade --noinput` once before web/worker come up — gate it via initContainers or a top-level Job with appropriate ordering.
- Readiness: web `httpGet /_health/ port=9000`, snuba-api `httpGet /health port=1218`, postgres `pg_isready`, clickhouse `httpGet /ping port=8123`, redis `redis-cli PING`, kafka `tcpSocket port=9092`.

## Output
One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder secret first. Add a top-level comment block above the secret explaining the upgrade Job + post-install user creation step.

SigNoz ({{instance}}) — OpenTelemetry-based observability (APM, metrics, logs, traces) backed by ClickHouse. ## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- ClickHouse PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Install via the official Helm chart `signoz/signoz`.
- Expose ONLY the SigNoz UI/API: `signoz.ingress.enabled: true`, `signoz.ingress.className: traefik`, `signoz.ingress.hosts: [{host: {{hostname}}, paths: [{path: /, pathType: Prefix}]}]`; `signoz.ingress.annotations` include `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure` and `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`; `signoz.ingress.tls: [{secretName: {{instance}}-tls, hosts: [{{hostname}}]}]`.
- Keep `otelCollector.ingress.enabled: false` — note in a comment that the OTLP ingest ports (4317 gRPC, 4318 HTTP) stay internal and can be exposed via a Tailscale Service if remote ingestion is needed.
- Single-node trim: run one replica of the SigNoz server and the otel-collector; keep the bundled `clickhouse` (with its ZooKeeper) and `telemetryStoreMigrator` enabled (`clickhouse.enabled: true`). Do NOT enable `postgresql`, `redpanda` or `signoz-otel-gateway` — they are not needed for a single-cluster install.
- Persistence: size the ClickHouse PVC at `{{storage}}Gi` on the default storageClass; leave the SigNoz server's small SQLite PVC at the chart default.
- Do NOT override image tags — use the chart's pinned defaults (appVersion v0.126.1).
- If {{nodeName}} is non-empty, set a matching `nodeSelector` on the SigNoz, otel-collector and ClickHouse components.

## Output
This app installs via its official Helm chart. Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml block holding a tuned `values.yaml`. SigNoz needs no secrets by default, so omit the ```secrets block unless {{notes}} calls for one. Do NOT emit helm commands and do NOT emit apiVersion/kind resources — the app runs Helm itself from the catalog's chart coordinates.

- 2 replicas, `RollingUpdate` (maxSurge 1, maxUnavailable 0) — it's stateless.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 101`, `runAsGroup: 101`, `fsGroup: 101` (nginx user inside the image). Container: `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, drop all caps.

- Container port 80 (the official image's nginx listens on 80, not 8080).

- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: requests 25m / 32Mi, limits 200m / 128Mi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: excalidraw`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Readiness + liveness probe: `httpGet / port=80`, initialDelaySeconds 3.

- Env on every postiz-* pod: pull `DATABASE_URL`, `REDIS_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` from the secret. Plus `FRONTEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=https://{{hostname}}/api`, `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://postiz-app:3000`, `STORAGE_PROVIDER=local`, `UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `NEXT_PUBLIC_UPLOAD_DIRECTORY=/uploads`, `MAIN_URL=https://{{hostname}}`.

- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000` for postiz pods.
- Uploads PVC mounted at `/uploads` on app + worker.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `postiz-app` exposes ports 3000 (frontend) and 3001 (backend) — Postiz combines both behind a single nginx layer in the image; ingress points at port 4200 (the bundled router) or whichever the image exposes by default; verify against the upstream Dockerfile.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Resources: app 300m/1500m, 512Mi/1.5Gi. worker + cron each 200m/1000m, 256Mi/1Gi.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: postiz`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|worker|cron|postgres|redis>`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Init Job: `pnpm run prisma-db-push` to migrate the schema before app starts.
- Readiness: app `httpGet / port=4200` initialDelaySeconds 30 (the app boots slowly).

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  MINIO_ROOT_USER: <FILL_ME_IN>
  MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  ports:
    - name: s3
      port: 9000
      targetPort: 9000
    - name: console
      port: 9001
      targetPort: 9001
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        runAsGroup: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: minio
          image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-04-22T22-12-26Z
          # First element is the real `minio` binary (image Cmd is ["minio"]);
          # `server` is a minio subcommand, so naming the binary first is required.
          command: ["minio", "server", "/data", "--console-address", ":9001"]
          env:
            - name: MINIO_ROOT_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MINIO_ROOT_USER
            - name: MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD
          ports:
            - name: s3
              containerPort: 9000
            - name: console
              containerPort: 9001
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /minio/health/live
              port: 9000
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 2Gi
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: minio
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    # Routes to the web console (9001). To expose the S3 API (9000) externally,
    # add a second host block below pointing at port 9000, e.g. s3.{{hostname}}.
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 9001

secrets.label: Root access key (username)
secrets.description: The MinIO root user / S3 access key you log into the console with and use as the S3 access key ID. Operator-chosen, e.g. a strong unique string.

secrets.label: Root secret key (password)
secrets.description: The MinIO root password / S3 secret key. App-generated strong secret.

description: The reference OCI/Docker registry (distribution) for pushing and pulling private container images. Minimal and fast - ideal as an in-cluster registry for your own builds.
notes: Add htpasswd auth before exposing it - an open registry is a serious risk. The {{storage}}Gi PVC stores all image layers; clients require HTTPS, which the Ingress provides.
installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Docker Registry** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Docker Registry ({{instance}}) - private OCI image registry.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `registry:2`, 1 replica, `Recreate`.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, `runAsUser: 1000`, `fsGroup: 1000`.
- PVC `{{storage}}Gi` at `/var/lib/registry`.
- Secret `{{instance}}-auth`: an `htpasswd` file (bcrypt) mounted at `/auth/htpasswd` (`<FILL_ME_IN>` - generate with `htpasswd -Bbn user pass`).
- Env: `REGISTRY_AUTH=htpasswd`, `REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_REALM=Registry`, `REGISTRY_AUTH_HTPASSWD_PATH=/auth/htpasswd`, `REGISTRY_HTTP_SECRET` (random, from secret).
- Container port 5000.
- Service: ClusterIP, port 80 -> targetPort 5000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`. Add annotation `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares` may need a large-body buffer; comment that pushes need a high `proxy-body-size`.
- Labels: every resource gets `app.kubernetes.io/name: docker-registry` and `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]` (cluster-wide convention).
- Readiness probe: `httpGet /v2/ port=5000` (401 is OK), or a `tcpSocket: 5000` check; initialDelaySeconds 10.

## Output
Reply with ONE fenced ```yaml code block containing the full multi-document manifest. No prose outside the fence. It must apply cleanly with `kubectl apply -f -`.

install.values:
expose:
  type: ingress
  tls:
    enabled: true
    certSource: secret
    secret:
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  ingress:
    hosts:
      core: {{hostname}}
    className: traefik
    annotations:
      cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
      traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
    labels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: harbor
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: portal
externalURL: https://{{hostname}}
harborAdminPassword: <FILL_ME_IN>
secretKey: <FILL_ME_IN>
persistence:
  enabled: true
  resourcePolicy: keep
  persistentVolumeClaim:
    registry:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: {{storage}}Gi
    jobservice:
      jobLog:
        accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
        size: 1Gi
    database:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
    redis:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
    trivy:
      accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
      size: 5Gi
  imageChartStorage:
    type: filesystem
    filesystem:
      rootdirectory: /storage
database:
  type: internal
redis:
  type: internal
trivy:
  enabled: true

secrets.label: Harbor admin password
secrets.description: Password for the built-in 'admin' user. Used to log into the Harbor UI and as the registry credential for docker login.

secrets.label: Encryption secret key
secrets.description: Key used by Harbor to encrypt sensitive data at rest. MUST be exactly 16 characters.

name: Harbor
tagline: Enterprise container registry with scanning and RBAC.

notes: The heaviest entry in the catalog (8+ components: core, portal, registry, jobservice, Trivy, Postgres, Redis). Installed through the official Helm chart rather than raw manifests.

description: Self-hosted GitLab Community Edition via the all-in-one Omnibus image - Git hosting, merge requests, CI/CD pipelines, a container registry, and issue tracking in a single container with bundled Postgres and Redis. Heavy; best on a node with spare CPU and 8GB+ free memory.

installPromptTemplate:
Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **GitLab CE** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
GitLab Community Edition ({{instance}}) - self-hosted DevOps platform (Git hosting, CI/CD, issues, container registry) via the all-in-one Omnibus image.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Data PVC: {{storage}}Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Single Deployment, image `gitlab/gitlab-ce:17.5.1-ce.0`, 1 replica, Recreate strategy. This is heavy and slow to boot (several minutes on first start).
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets` (stringData) with `root-password: "<FILL_ME_IN>"`. Reference it as env `GITLAB_ROOT_PASSWORD` via secretKeyRef - do NOT inline the password anywhere else.
- Env `GITLAB_OMNIBUS_CONFIG` (a multi-line block scalar) sets at least: `external_url 'https://{{hostname}}'`; `gitlab_rails['initial_root_password'] = ENV['GITLAB_ROOT_PASSWORD']`; `nginx['listen_port'] = 80`; `nginx['listen_https'] = false` (TLS terminates at traefik); `gitlab_rails['gitlab_shell_ssh_port'] = 22`. Tune for a small single-node instance: `puma['worker_processes'] = 2`, `sidekiq['max_concurrency'] = 10`, `prometheus_monitoring['enable'] = false`.
- Ports: 80 (HTTP), 22 (SSH).
- PVCs (default storageClass): `{{storage}}Gi` mounted at `/var/opt/gitlab` (data), plus a 1Gi PVC at `/etc/gitlab` (config).
- Service: ClusterIP exposing 80 and 22 (SSH is not reachable from outside the cluster without a NodePort or LoadBalancer - mention in a comment but do not add one).
- Ingress: port 80 only. `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS: `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: gitlab`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`.
- `imagePullSecrets: [{name: {{imagePullSecret}}}]`.
- Probes (GitLab boots slowly - be generous): startupProbe `httpGet /-/health` on port 80 with `failureThreshold: 60`, `periodSeconds: 10`; readinessProbe `httpGet /-/readiness` on port 80, `initialDelaySeconds: 60`. Apply the requests/limits above.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-secrets
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
type: Opaque
stringData:
  SECRET_KEY: <FILL_ME_IN>
  UTILS_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  POSTGRES_PASSWORD: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_CLIENT_ID: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_AUTH_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_TOKEN_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_USERINFO_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
  OIDC_LOGOUT_URI: <FILL_ME_IN>
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-data
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: {{storage}}Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 5Gi
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  ports:
    - name: postgres
      port: 5432
      targetPort: 5432
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
spec:
  serviceName: postgres
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: postgres
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
        fsGroup: 999
      containers:
        - name: postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_USER
              value: outline
            - name: POSTGRES_DB
              value: outline
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: PGDATA
              value: /var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5432
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["pg_isready", "-U", "outline", "-d", "outline"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/postgresql/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-postgres
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  ports:
    - name: redis
      port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: redis
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
spec:
  serviceName: redis
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: redis
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 999
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: redis:7-alpine
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["redis-cli", "PING"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}-migrate
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
spec:
  backoffLimit: 3
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: migrate
    spec:
      restartPolicy: OnFailure
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1001
      initContainers:
        - name: wait-postgres
          image: postgres:16-alpine

            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
      containers:
        - name: migrate
          image: outlinewiki/outline:latest

          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: DATABASE_URL

            - name: PGSSLMODE
              value: disable
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY
            - name: UTILS_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: UTILS_SECRET
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app

  selector:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app

---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  strategy:
    type: Recreate
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
      app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
        app.kubernetes.io/component: app
    spec:
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1001
        fsGroup: 1001
      containers:
        - name: outline
          image: outlinewiki/outline:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
            - name: URL
              value: https://{{hostname}}
            - name: PORT
              value: "3000"
            - name: NODE_ENV
              value: production
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              value: postgres://outline:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/outline
            - name: PGSSLMODE

            - name: REDIS_URL
              value: redis://redis:6379
            - name: FILE_STORAGE
              value: local
            - name: FILE_STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT_DIR
              value: /var/lib/outline/data
            - name: FILE_STORAGE_UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE
              value: "26214400"
            - name: FORCE_HTTPS
              value: "false"
            - name: SECRET_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: SECRET_KEY
            - name: UTILS_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: UTILS_SECRET
            - name: OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME
              value: authentik
            - name: OIDC_USERNAME_CLAIM
              value: preferred_username
            - name: OIDC_SCOPES

            - name: OIDC_CLIENT_ID
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_CLIENT_ID
            - name: OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET
            - name: OIDC_AUTH_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_AUTH_URI
            - name: OIDC_TOKEN_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_TOKEN_URI
            - name: OIDC_USERINFO_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_USERINFO_URI
            - name: OIDC_LOGOUT_URI
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: {{instance}}-secrets
                  key: OIDC_LOGOUT_URI
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 512Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 1000m
              memory: 1Gi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /_health
              port: 3000
            initialDelaySeconds: 20
            periodSeconds: 10
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            capabilities:
              drop: [ALL]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/outline/data
      volumes:
        - name: data
          persistentVolumeClaim:
            claimName: {{instance}}-data
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: {{instance}}
  namespace: {{namespace}}
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/name: outline
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}
    app.kubernetes.io/component: app
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}
spec:
  ingressClassName: traefik
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - {{hostname}}
      secretName: {{instance}}-tls
  rules:
    - host: {{hostname}}
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: {{instance}}
                port:
                  number: 3000

name: Outline
tagline: Team knowledge base and wiki.
description: Fast, collaborative team wiki and documentation tool - markdown editing, real-time collaboration, nested collections, full-text search, and a slick reader UI. Stack: app server (Node), Postgres, Redis, plus file storage (local PVC or S3). Requires an external SSO provider for login.
notes: Outline has NO built-in email/password login - it requires an external authentication provider (generic OIDC, Google, Slack, or Azure AD). The self-hosted Authentik already in this catalog can act as the OIDC provider. There is no official Helm chart, so this installs from raw manifests with bundled Postgres + Redis and local file storage.

Generate a Kubernetes manifest for **Outline** ({{instance}}) on the user's k3s cluster.

## App
Outline ({{instance}}) - self-hosted team knowledge base / wiki.

## Target
- Namespace: {{namespace}}
- Ingress hostname: {{hostname}}
- Node pin: {{nodeName}} (empty = no nodeSelector)
- Data PVC: {{storage}}Gi; Postgres PVC: 5Gi
- Notes: {{notes}}

## Conventions
- Workloads: `outline` (Deployment, image `outlinewiki/outline:latest`, listens on 3000), `postgres` (StatefulSet, `postgres:16-alpine`), `redis` (StatefulSet, `redis:7-alpine`). 1 replica each, Recreate.
- Secret `{{instance}}-secrets` (stringData): `SECRET_KEY` and `UTILS_SECRET` (each a 32-byte hex `<FILL_ME_IN>`, i.e. `openssl rand -hex 32`), `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`), and the SSO provider credentials `OIDC_CLIENT_ID` + `OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET` (`<FILL_ME_IN>`). Reference everything via secretKeyRef - never inline secrets.
- Env on the app: `URL=https://{{hostname}}`, `PORT=3000`, `NODE_ENV=production`, `DATABASE_URL=postgres://outline:$(POSTGRES_PASSWORD)@postgres:5432/outline`, `PGSSLMODE=disable`, `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379`, `FILE_STORAGE=local`, `FILE_STORAGE_LOCAL_ROOT_DIR=/var/lib/outline/data`, `FILE_STORAGE_UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE=26214400`, `FORCE_HTTPS=false` (TLS terminates at traefik).
- Auth (generic OIDC, e.g. pointed at the cluster's Authentik): set `OIDC_AUTH_URI`, `OIDC_TOKEN_URI`, `OIDC_USERINFO_URI`, `OIDC_LOGOUT_URI` and `OIDC_DISPLAY_NAME` as plain env to `<FILL_ME_IN>`; pull `OIDC_CLIENT_ID`/`OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET` from the Secret. Note in a comment that the provider's redirect URI is `https://{{hostname}}/auth/oidc.callback`.
- An init Job (image `outlinewiki/outline:latest`, command `yarn db:migrate`) runs before the app pod to apply migrations; keep it idempotent.
- PVCs (default storageClass): app data `{{storage}}Gi` at `/var/lib/outline/data`, Postgres 5Gi.
- Pod security: `runAsNonRoot: true`, app runs as uid 1001.
- Services: all ClusterIP. `outline` exposes port 3000.
- Ingress: `ingressClassName: traefik`. Annotations: `cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: {{clusterIssuer}}`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.entrypoints: web,websecure`, `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.middlewares: {{redirectMiddleware}}`. TLS `secretName: {{instance}}-tls`.
- Labels: `app.kubernetes.io/name: outline`, `app.kubernetes.io/instance: {{instance}}`, `app.kubernetes.io/component: <app|postgres|redis>`.
- Resources: app 250m/1000m, 512Mi/1Gi.
- Readiness: app `httpGet /_health port=3000` initialDelaySeconds 20.

One fenced ```yaml block, multi-document, placeholder Secret first. No prose outside the fence.
